title: vicia graminea var. setifolia
intro: Vicia graminea var. setifolia, also known as hairy vetch or hairy purple vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its seeds which contain a compound called robinin, a type of flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The seeds of Vicia graminea var. setifolia have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, due to their ability to soothe and calm the body. This plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically used to promote overall health and well-being.
